paper_title: More Data, Worse Models: Non-Monotonic Scaling under Long-Tail Label Noise

issues_found.what_extractor_says: source_section is '7 Limitations: Cross-sectional pilot / Appendix D' and source_text contains 'No experiment was re-run after initial reporting; reported numbers reflect the first-and-only completed run per configuration.'
issues_found.what_paper_says: The 'Cross-sectional pilot' subsection of Section 7 (p.15) only states 'Our training runs are conducted as a single, per-configuration optimisation to convergence; we do not study multi-pass effects across training epochs, and we do not examine the interaction of the crossover with ongoing curriculum learning or self-training strategies.' The sentence about 'No experiment was re-run after initial reporting; reported numbers reflect the first-and-only completed run per configuration' is found ONLY in Appendix D (p.18), not in Section 7. Splicing across sections is acknowledged in source_section but the spliced quote is presented as one continuous source_text.
issues_found.evidence_quote: Appendix D, p.18: 'No experiment was re-run after initial reporting; reported numbers reflect the first-and-only completed run per configuration.' Section 7 Cross-sectional pilot, p.15: 'Our training runs are conducted as a single, per-configuration optimisation to convergence; we do not study multi-pass effects across training epochs...'

issues_found.what_extractor_says: name field is 'Theorem 4: Crossover condition (eq. 5)' â€” labels the crossover-condition theorem as Theorem 4.
issues_found.what_paper_says: The PDF (p.10) does indeed label the crossover-condition statement 'Theorem 4 (Crossover condition)'. This is verified, but it is somewhat unusual numbering â€” the only other numbered theorem is Theorem 1 and the auxiliary results are Lemma 2 and Lemma 3 (no Theorems 2 or 3 visible). The extraction faithfully reproduces the PDF's idiosyncratic numbering, so this is NOT an error â€” flagging only as a cross-check confirmation that the 'Theorem 4' label was deliberately copied from the source rather than a typo.

summary.notes: Extraction is highly faithful to the PDF on all five focus areas: (1) specific numbers (n*=1.2M, 34.7â†’38.2%) cell-correct; (2) ResNet-50/152/ViT-B/16 results not mixed up â€” Table 3 mapping preserved; (3) tail noise Î·_tail=0.28 correctly higher than head Î·_head=0.05 throughout; (4) Theorem 1 (PAC-Bayes bound, eq. 3) and Theorem 4 (crossover condition, eq. 5) are kept as distinct equations with correct LaTeX; (5) Hestness, Kaplan, Hoffmann all present in paper_references â€” only Henighan (ref [3]) is missing from the extracted reference list, a minor omission. The single section-attribution issue (exist11 spliced from Section 7 + Appendix D) is acknowledged in the source_section field. No high-severity misattributions found.
